title: AI needs a strong data fabric to deliver business value
optimized_headline: How a Robust Data Fabric Unlocks AI's True Business Potential
raw_excerpt: Artificial intelligence is moving quickly in the enterprise, from experimentation to everyday use. Organizations are deploying copilots, agents, and predictive systems across finance, supply chains, human resources, and customer operations. By the end of 2025, half of companies used AI in at least three business functions, according to a recent survey. But as AI becomes…
raw_body: Artificial intelligence is moving quickly in the enterprise, from experimentation to everyday use. Organizations are deploying copilots, agents, and predictive systems across finance, supply chains, human resources, and customer operations. By the end of 2025, half of companies used AI in at least three business functions, according to a recent survey. But as AI becomes…
speedrun: Artificial intelligence is rapidly transitioning from experimental projects to practical applications in businesses. A recent survey predicts that by the end of 2025, half of all companies will implement AI in at least three different functions. This shift highlights the increasing reliance on AI to streamline operations in areas like finance and customer service. Understanding how to manage data effectively is crucial for maximizing the value of these AI initiatives now.
why_it_matters: Businesses that adopt AI could see improved efficiency and decision-making across multiple departments, enhancing overall productivity.
This trend signals a broader shift in the market towards integrated AI solutions, making data management essential for competitive advantage.

lenses.eli12: AI is becoming a regular part of how businesses operate, moving beyond testing to real-world applications. Imagine AI as a helpful assistant in various departments, like finance or HR, making tasks easier. This matters because it can help companies work smarter and faster, benefiting everyday people through better services.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this trend indicates a growing user need for AI-driven solutions across multiple business functions. It also highlights the importance of cost-effective data management to ensure these tools deliver real value. A practical implication could be the need to invest in robust data infrastructures to support AI initiatives.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the shift to widespread AI use emphasizes the need for a strong data fabric to support various models and applications. The survey indicates that by 2025, half of companies will utilize AI in multiple functions, which necessitates efficient data handling to optimize performance. Ensuring data quality and accessibility will be critical in realizing the full potential of AI technologies.
